g.data-narration: Welcome! Today we learn when constructors and destructors execute in C++ - crucial for exam success.
g.text: When Constructors and Destructors Are Executed
university 2022 Scheme - Object-Oriented Programming with C++

g.data-narration: Constructors initialize objects when created, destructors clean up when objects are destroyed. They're automatic!
C++ has four main object types: global, local, static, and dynamic. Each has different execution timing.
g.text: Four Types of Objects in C++

g.data-narration: Global objects are declared outside functions. They're constructed before main() starts and destroyed after main() ends.
g.text: Global Objects: Before main() → After main()

g.data-narration: Local objects are created when execution reaches their declaration and destroyed when the block exits, in reverse order.
Static local objects are constructed only once on first function call and destroyed when main() terminates.
g.text: Static Objects: Constructed Once, Destroyed at Program End
→ Destructor called when main() ends (after all static objects)

g.data-narration: Dynamic objects created with new persist until explicitly deleted. Without delete, destructors never run - causing memory leaks!
g.text: Dynamic Objects: Manual Lifetime Control with new/delete

g.data-narration: In inheritance, base constructors run before derived constructors. Destructors run in reverse - derived first, then base.
g.text: Destructors run in REVERSE: Derived → Base

g.data-narration: Member objects are constructed before their container class, in declaration order - NOT initializer list order!
g.text: ⚠ Note: Initializer list order doesn't matter - declaration order is used!

g.data-narration: Virtual base classes are constructed before non-virtual bases and only once, regardless of multiple inheritance paths.
g.text: Virtual Base Classes: Constructed Once, Before Non-Virtual
class D : public B, public C { };

g.data-narration: Array elements are constructed in order 0,1,2... but destructors run in reverse: last element first!
g.text: Arrays of Objects: Construct Forward, Destroy Backward
delete[] arr; // ← Note: delete[] not delete!

g.data-narration: Remember these four golden rules: reverse order, base before derived, declaration order, virtual base first.
Avoid these exam mistakes: missing virtual destructors, wrong delete for arrays, wrong member order assumption.
g.text: Common Mistakes - Avoid These in Exams!

g.data-narration: For university exams, focus on predicting output - trace constructors then trace destructors in reverse. Draw timelines!
You now understand object lifecycle! constructors initialize, destructors cleanup, and order follows specific rules for exam success.
g.text: Constructors Initialize • Destructors Cleanup • Order Matters!
Practice predicting output for inheritance and member objects
Remember: Base → Derived, Reverse Order for destruction
